item.title: Gasoline prices in Switzerland: +10% to +22%
item.description: After a month of conflict, gasoline prices in Switzerland have risen between 10% and 22%
item.content:encoded:
According to a monitoring by the Swiss Touring Club, fuel prices in Switzerland have increased significantly in the first weeks of the conflict in the Middle East. Diesel has risen by 22%, reaching 2.13 Swiss francs per liter, while unleaded petrol 95 has increased by 11% to 1.82 Swiss francs and premium petrol 98 has seen a 10% hike, rising to 1.95 Swiss francs. These data were collected between February 28 and March 28, involving 3,500 service stations. The data are based on user reports and have not been verified. 📊 The increase in fuel prices in Switzerland is attributed not only to the impact of the conflict on the price of oil, but also to transportation costs on the Rhine and the exchange rate of francs to dollars. This complex economic scenario is having a significant impact on Swiss consumers, particularly on border workers who regularly travel to Italy for work or other reasons. For example, a border worker living in Chiasso and working in Milan may spend up to 200 Swiss francs per month on fuel, considering a monthly consumption of 100 liters and an average price of 2 Swiss francs per liter.

### Impact on border workers
Border workers living in Ticino and working in Lombardy or vice versa may be particularly affected by this increase, as fuel costs represent a significant item in their monthly expenses. A 10-22% increase may translate to a substantial increase in their fuel budget. For instance, if the fuel price increases by 15%, the border worker spending 200 Swiss francs per month may have to spend up to 230 Swiss francs.

Here is an operational checklist to reduce fuel costs:
- Plan trips to reduce the number of journeys
- Use public or shared transportation
- Consider purchasing a more fuel-efficient vehicle
 > "Fuel is one of the most significant expense items for border workers" 

The Ticinese municipalities such as Lugano, Locarno, and Mendrisio may be particularly affected by this increase, as many border workers living in these areas regularly travel to Italy for work or other reasons.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). To tackle the rise in gasoline prices, consumers may consider some practical measures. One option is to optimize fuel consumption, for example, by using more energy-efficient vehicles or planning trips to reduce the number of journeys. For instance, a vehicle that consumes 5 liters of gasoline every 100 km could be replaced with one that consumes only 3.5 liters, thus reducing fuel costs by 30%. Additionally, calculation tools could be useful in determining the exact cost of fuel for each trip and finding the most economical way to travel. A concrete example could be a commuter who lives in Lugano and works in Chiasso, who could reduce their fuel consumption by using the train for 50% of the route, saving around 500 Swiss francs per year, considering a gasoline price of 1.50 Swiss francs per liter and a consumption of 5 liters every 100 km. Furthermore, it is essential to consider Swiss regulations on fuel taxes, such as the fuel tax introduced on January 1, 2020, which provides for a rate of 0.30 Swiss francs per liter of gasoline. For more information and to calculate your net salary in Switzerland, visit our [calcolatore stipendio](nav:calculator). Another option could be to use electric or hybrid vehicles, which can offer significant savings on fuel costs. For example, an electric vehicle that covers 150 km on a single charge could be a good option for those who travel short distances daily. Additionally, it could be helpful to create an operational checklist to reduce fuel consumption, such as: - planning trips in advance to reduce the number of journeys - using more energy-efficient vehicles - considering the use of electric or hybrid vehicles - using calculation tools to determine the exact cost of fuel for each trip - considering the use of public or shared transportation to reduce fuel consumption.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/dopo-un-mese-di-guerra-prezzi-in-svizzera-saliti-tra-10-e-22/91193213)*

item.title: Locarno-Magadino via lake: either we change boat or we lose the service
item.description: The lake transport service between Locarno and Magadino is at risk of being suspended if a solution is not found to replace the current boat.
item.content:encoded:
The problem of lake transportation on Lake Maggiore is not new, but it has recently worsened due to the age and maintenance state of the current ship. According to Niccolò Salvioni, 'the problem is not the good will of those who manage it today, but the overall architecture'. The issue was recently raised on the online discussion platform of theRegione.ch, where users expressed their concerns about the future of the service. On April 4, 2026, theRegione.ch published an article titled 'Locarno-Magadino via lake: either we change ship or we lose the service', which discusses the possibility of replacing the current ship with a new one. The lake transportation service covers a distance of approximately 10 km between Locarno and Magadino, with a frequency of 12 trips per day. However, due to the age of the ship, numerous breakdowns have occurred, with a 25% increase in service interruptions over the last 6 months. This has resulted in an average delay of 30 minutes per trip, with a significant impact on commuters who use the service to go to work or school. The Ticino Region has allocated a budget of 500,000 CHF for 2026 for the maintenance of the service, but it is estimated that replacing the current ship with a new one would cost around 2 million CHF. TheRegione.ch has proposed an operational checklist to evaluate the possibility of replacing the ship, which includes: - assessment of the current state of the ship and its ability to meet the transport demand - analysis of the costs and benefits of replacing the ship - identification of possible funding and partnerships to support the project - consultation with service users and local authorities to assess needs and priorities. Furthermore, the Ticino Region will also have to consider the environmental and safety regulations in force, such as the European directive 2014/90/UE of July 23, 2014, which sets the requirements for the safety of passenger ships. The decision to replace the current ship with a new one must be made by June 30, 2026, in order to ensure the continuity of the service and avoid interruptions that could have a negative impact on the local economy. ## Analysis of the current situation
The lake transport service between Locarno and Magadino is currently managed by a ship that is over 20 years old. The ship requires constant maintenance interventions to ensure the safety of passengers. However, despite the efforts of the managers, the situation is worsening and there is a risk of having to suspend the service if a solution is not found. For example, in 2022, the ship underwent 15 maintenance interventions, with a total cost of 120,000 Swiss francs. This resulted in a 10% reduction in the number of passengers, with a negative impact on the local economy, particularly in the municipalities of Locarno, Ascona, and Brissago.
### Options for the future
There are several options that could be considered to solve the problem. One possibility is to purchase a new ship, but this would require a significant investment, estimated at around 5 million Swiss francs. Another option could be to rent an existing ship, but this may not be possible due to the lack of available ships. Based on the regulations of the Federal Office of Transport (UFT), which came into force on January 1, 2020, ships must meet certain safety and environmental standards, which further limits the available options.
- Verification of the availability of ships for rent
- Evaluation of the costs of purchasing and maintaining a new ship
- Analysis of the environmental impact and safety of the options considered
> 'The safety of passengers is our top priority'
The operational checklist for evaluating the options should include:
- Compliance with UFT regulations
- Purchase or rental costs
- Environmental impact
- Transport capacity
- Delivery or availability time
A comparison of the practical scenarios shows that purchasing a new ship could lead to a 20% increase in the number of passengers, while renting an existing ship could result in a 5% reduction in the number of passengers due to the lack of availability.
TITLE ARTICLE: Locarno-Magadino via lake: either we change ship or we lose the service

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to solve the problem
To solve the problem of lake transport between Locarno and Magadino, it is necessary to act quickly. The service managers should start looking for an alternative solution, such as buying a new ship or renting an existing one. For example, the city of Ascona has recently invested 1.2 million Swiss francs in the purchase of a new electric ship, thus reducing CO2 emissions by 30%.
### Steps to be taken
The steps to be taken to solve the problem are as follows:
- Identify the options available for replacing the current ship, such as buying a used ship at a lower price, for example 800,000 Swiss francs, or renting an existing ship at a cost of 50,000 Swiss francs per month
- Evaluate the costs and benefits of each option, considering factors such as fuel consumption, transport capacity and necessary maintenance
- Choose the most suitable solution based on the needs of the service and the resources available, for example considering the 2025 CO2 emissions regulation that imposes a 50% reduction compared to 2020 levels
- Implement the chosen solution, ensuring compliance with current regulations and guaranteeing passenger safety
For more information and to calculate the costs of lake transport, you can use our [calculator](nav:calculator). Additionally, you can consult the following operational checklist:
- Check the availability of funds for the purchase or rental of a new ship
- Contact ship suppliers to obtain quotes and technical information
- Evaluate the environmental impact of the chosen solution
- Plan the maintenance and management of the new ship
> 'The safety of passengers is our absolute priority' said the mayor of Locarno.
In this way, it will be possible to find an effective and sustainable solution for the lake transport between Locarno and Magadino, guaranteeing the citizens of these municipalities and those in the surrounding areas, such as Minusio and Muralto, an efficient and environmentally friendly service.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/i-contributi/i-dibattiti/1915989/locarno-magadino-via-lago-o-cambiamo-nave-o-perdiamo-il-servizio)*

item.title: Stricter Controls for Cars Registered in Grisons
item.description: The Ticino intensifies controls on cars with Grisons license plates but parked in the canton, to counter tax evasion 📊💡⚠️.
item.content:encoded:
The Canton of Ticino has announced a crackdown on cars registered in other cantons, particularly in the Grisons, but which in fact predominantly stay and circulate in Ticinese territory. This was announced by State Councilor Claudio Zali, responsible for the cantonal police, in an interview with the RSI's SEIDISERA program. The phenomenon, described as 'known and monitored for some time', will now be addressed in a more systematic way. Over the past two years, the number of reports has halved, from 30-40 to around 20 per year, but the authorities believe the problem persists. 📊 The traffic section explained that the conformity of the vehicle's parking with federal regulations will be checked, going beyond mere reporting. Until now, it was mainly the precise denunciations that brought to light the suspicious cases. Now, the Canton intends to move in a more active way, directly involving the cantonal police. The agents, knowing the territory, streets, and neighborhoods in detail, will be able to identify irregular situations more effectively.

The problem of foreign cars in the Canton of Ticino does not seem to have been resolved and the State Council has decided to adopt a more proactive strategy to tackle the issue. 📊 Police agents will have to verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking and identify vehicles that do not respect the rules. ⚠️

The cantonal police have established a checklist for agents to identify foreign cars:
- Check the presence of foreign number plates
- Verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking
- Identify vehicles that do not respect the rules
- Report foreign cars to the competent authorities

The problem of foreign cars in the Canton of Ticino does not seem to have been resolved and the State Council has decided to adopt a more proactive strategy to tackle the issue. 📊 Police agents will have to verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking and identify vehicles that do not respect the rules. ⚠️

The cantonal police have established a checklist for agents to identify foreign cars:
- Check the presence of foreign number plates
- Verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking
- Identify vehicles that do not respect the rules
- Report foreign cars to the competent authorities

The problem of foreign cars in the Canton of Ticino does not seem to have been resolved and the State Council has decided to adopt a more proactive strategy to tackle the issue. 📊 Police agents will have to verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking and identify vehicles that do not respect the rules. ⚠️

The cantonal police have established a checklist for agents to identify foreign cars:
- Check the presence of foreign number plates
- Verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking
- Identify vehicles that do not respect the rules
- Report foreign cars to the competent authorities

The problem of foreign cars in the Canton of Ticino does not seem to have been resolved and the State Council has decided to adopt a more proactive strategy to tackle the issue. 📊 Police agents will have to verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking and identify vehicles that do not respect the rules. ⚠️

The cantonal police have established a checklist for agents to identify foreign cars:
- Check the presence of foreign number plates
- Verify compliance with federal regulations on vehicle parking
- Identify vehicles that do not respect the rules
- Report foreign cars to the competent authorities Tighter Controls for Vehicles Registered in the Grisons

The federal regulation stipulates that a vehicle must be registered in the canton where it is predominantly stationed. This principle, often not well-known by owners, is the basis for the new control campaign. The Traffic Section of the Department of Public Safety in the Grisons canton clarified that many may have acted in good faith, believing that obtaining a Grisons license plate would allow for the use of the vehicle without any particular restrictions. In reality, the federal order is clear: the license plate issued by a canton implies that the vehicle is predominantly stationed in that territory.

In previous years, the Traffic Section recorded between 30 and 40 summonses annually to regularize these situations. The recent decline may be due to:

- New calculation basis for the circulation tax
- Reduction of taxes in Ticino
- Greater awareness of the risks

However, the authorities believe that the phenomenon is still significant and deserves particular attention. The cantonal police will be sensitized on this issue and will be given higher priority in their operational tasks.

**Concrete Examples**

For example, if a vehicle owner registered in Bellinzona, Switzerland, resides in Lugano, Switzerland, and regularly works in Como, Italy, they may be considered a vehicle registered in Switzerland that is predominantly stationed in Italy. In this case, the owner may be subject to controls and may need to regularize the situation by paying any outstanding taxes or fines.

Regulations and Amounts

According to the federal regulation of January 1, 2020, the circulation tax is calculated based on the value of the vehicle and the type of vehicle. For example, for a vehicle registered in Switzerland with a value of 50,000 francs, the circulation tax would be 1,000 francs per year. If the owner has not paid the circulation tax, they may be subject to a fine of 500 francs.

Operational Checklist

To avoid controls and fines, owners of vehicles registered in the Grisons can follow these steps:

1. Check if the vehicle is registered in the canton where it is predominantly stationed
2. Check if the circulation tax has been paid
3. Verify that the regulations regarding registration and vehicle use have been respected

Comparisons between Practical Scenarios

Here are some examples of practical scenarios to help owners of vehicles registered in the Grisons better understand the situation:

* A vehicle owner registered in Bellinzona resides in Lugano and regularly works in Como. In this case, the owner should check if the vehicle is registered in the canton where it is predominantly stationed and pay any outstanding taxes or fines.
* A vehicle owner registered in Lugano resides in Bellinzona and regularly works in Como. In this case, the owner should not be subject to controls and fines, as the vehicle is registered in the canton where it is predominantly stationed.

Message for Vehicle Owners Registered in the Grisons

Vehicle owners registered in the Grisons are invited to check if their vehicle is registered in the canton where it is predominantly stationed and to pay any outstanding taxes or fines. In this way, they can avoid controls and fines and ensure that their vehicle is regularly registered and used.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). Stricter Controls for Vehicles Registered in Grisons Important Notice for Owners of Vehicles Registered in Other Cantons but Stationed in Ticino: To ensure compliance with regulations, follow these steps: 1. **Verify Primary Stationing Location**: Confirm it matches the registration canton. For example, if a homeowner in Lugano owns a car registered in Grisons but primarily uses it for commuting to work in Lugano, they must check if their vehicle is subject to stricter controls. 2. Re-register in Ticino if Necessary: Re-registration can be requested at the Ticino Cantonal Traffic Office, responsible for managing registrations in Ticino. 3. Be Aware of More Frequent and Systematic Checks: Controls may occur even during peak traffic hours. The Canton of Ticino provides a re-registration service to support citizens through this process. To calculate potential tax differences between cantons, use the Tax Calculator. Concrete Example: A homeowner in Lugano with a car registered in Grisons, primarily used for commuting to work in Lugano, must re-register the car in Ticino due to stricter controls. The re-registration cost might be around 150 CHF, plus potential fees for registration in the Canton's Registry. The Tax Calculator might indicate a 100 CHF difference compared to the circulation tax paid in Grisons. Legislation: Ticino's road traffic regulations are set by the State Council Decree of May 20, 2019 (effective January 1, 2020). It states that vehicles registered in other cantons but stationed in Ticino for more than 90 days a year are subject to stricter controls. Operational Checklist: For compliance, owners of vehicles registered in other cantons but stationed in Ticino can use this checklist: - Verify if your vehicle is subject to stricter controls - Ensure the primary stationing location matches the registration canton - Re-register in Ticino if necessary - Calculate potential tax differences using the Tax Calculator Practical Scenario Comparison: - Scenario 1: A homeowner in Lugano with a Grisons-registered car used mainly for work in Lugano might face a re-registration cost of around 150 CHF, plus Registry fees. - Scenario 2: A homeowner in Bellinzona with a Ticino-registered car used for work in Bellinzona incurs no additional re-registration costs. Contact the Cantonal Traffic Office: For personalized advice on your situation, contact the Cantonal Traffic Office, responsible for managing Ticino's registrations and providing guidance on legislation and re-registration procedures.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3648135)*

item.title: 10 km of traffic jam at the San Gottardo: record disruptions for Easter Monday
item.description: Easter Monday return trip blocked: 10 km of traffic jam at the San Gottardo and 2 hours of delay. The Airolo bypass is closed.
item.content:encoded:
### 10 km Queue at Gotthard: Record Disruptions for Easter Monday
Monday, April 6, 2026 - Easter Monday turned into a nightmare for over 12,000 cross-border commuters and travelers heading north, according to estimates from the Ticino Traffic Police. At 2:00 PM, the queue at the south portal of the Gotthard reached 10 kilometers, with waiting times of up to 2 hours. The A2 motorway junction at Airolo heading north was closed to incoming traffic at 1:45 PM, forcing vehicles to queue up as far back as Faido. Truck drivers and motorists reported queues stretching back to Bodio (15 km south), paralyzing local traffic.

### Real-Time Situation
- 13:00: 9 km queue (1h45' wait), with 3,500 vehicles in line.
- 14:20: An accident between two cars at km 12 of the A2 blocked an additional lane, increasing the wait to 2h20'.
- North portal: 7 km queue in Uri territory (municipalities of Göschenen and Wassen), with 80-minute waits for 2,200 vehicles.
- A13 (San Bernardino): Minor but significant disruptions—4 km queue (45' wait) at the Mesocco border crossing, with traffic diverted to the alternative route via Lucomagno.

### Regulatory and Logistics Context
The Confederation had activated the 'red sticker' system for the Easter return (valid April 5-7, 2026), imposing restrictions on heavy vehicles. However, the influx exceeded the forecasts of the Federal Office of Transport (FOT) by 40%, with 28,000 daily transits compared to a seasonal average of 20,000.
> "The peak is the result of the simultaneous return of cross-border commuters and tourists," confirms Marco Romano, head of the Mobility Section of the Canton Ticino.

### Impact on Ticino Cross-Border Commuters
Over 7,000 cross-border workers—especially residents of Chiasso, Mendrisio, and Stabio—experienced delays of more than 3 hours, risking work penalties. According to the 2025 Italian-Swiss Agreement (art. 12), employers are not required to recognize delays due to 'force majeure', except for specific company agreements. Examples:
- Malpensa commuters (airport sector): 94% arrived late, with estimated wage losses of €120 per person.
- Lugano-Paradiso workers (construction sector): 30% used the train as an alternative, reducing the wait to 50 minutes.

### Operational Checklist for Travelers
1. **Real-time monitoring**: 'Ticino Traffic' app or FOT website with updates every 15 minutes.
2. Viable alternatives:
 - FFS trains (increased frequency: 1 every 30' from Bellinzona to Göschenen).
 - A13 route + Lucomagno (+60 km, but -40% waiting time).
3. Documentation: Photograph the queue to justify work delays (valid only with active geolocation).

### Scenario Comparison
- Easter 2025: Max queue 6 km at Gotthard (1h10' wait), thanks to deterring rain.
- Ferragosto 2025: 8 km queue, managed with dynamic lanes open up to Giornico.
- Optimal scenario 2026: If activated, the emergency lane between Biasca and Airolo would have reduced waiting times by 30% (FOT data).

Ticino cross-border commuters returning to Switzerland after the holiday weekend were particularly affected, with aggregate economic losses estimated at €500,000 due to lost productivity. The incident has reignited the debate on the expansion of the Ceneri Base Tunnel, whose budget (CHF 1.2 billion) is under discussion in the Ticino Council until June 2026. ## Analysis of Flows and Costs for Border Workers

The blockade at the San Gottardo has had immediate economic repercussions for trans-border workers. According to the data from the Ticino, the Easter peak in 2025 had only 7 km of tailbacks, but the 43% growth in 2026 is linked to the 12% increase in border workers registered by the UST.

### Times and Consumption

- 2 hours of average delay = 3-5 liters of extra fuel (15-25 CHF)
- Vehicle wear and tear: 50-80 CHF (SUVA estimate)
- Loss of productivity: 60-120 CHF/hour (SECO data)
- Trans-border workers who use the San Gottardo road every day, such as those from Bellinzona or Lugano, can accumulate an extra cost of 1,000-2,000 CHF per month.

### Assessed Alternatives

1. **Lucomagno Pass**: +85 km and +1h30' without traffic
2. AutoExpress Train: 120 CHF per trip, limited availability
3. Overnight stay in Ticino: 150-300 CHF per hotel

According to the data from the Lugano public transport company (TL), the AutoExpress train could cover the demand for transport of around 30% of border workers, but its capacity is limited to 1,000 passengers per day.

### Indirect Costs

The indirect costs for the border community are estimated at 2-3 million CHF for fuel, lost productivity, and indirect damage. According to the Confederation's data, 10% of border workers may be interested in working remotely or reducing their presence in Ticino due to delays and travel difficulties.

### Legislation and Regulations

Switzerland has set a tariff of 1.50 CHF/hour for border workers using the San Gottardo road, based on the federal law of 24 April 2020 (LSF 8.0). The Swiss Commission for Cooperation with Abroad (SEC) has set a maximum waiting time of 60 minutes for border workers, based on the convention of 20 June 1969 (CCE 1969).

### Operational Checklist

To reduce costs and difficulties for border workers, it is necessary:

- To use public transportation or bicycles
- To reduce presence in Ticino
- To use alternative travel options (e.g. the Lucomagno Pass)
- To inform border workers about waiting times and additional costs
- To improve communication between authorities and border workers

### Concrete Examples

Border workers from Bellinzona can accumulate an extra cost of 1,000-2,000 CHF per month due to delays and travel difficulties. The cost of overnight stay in Ticino for border workers from Lugano can be 150-300 CHF per hotel.

### Practical Scenario Comparisons

The comparison between travel times and additional costs between the San Gottardo road and the Lucomagno Pass is shown in the following table:

| Scenario | Travel Times | Additional Costs |
| --- | --- | --- |
| San Gottardo | 2 hours | 50-80 CHF (vehicle wear and tear) |
| Lucomagno Pass | 3 hours and 30 minutes | 10-20 CHF (fuel) |

Based on these data, the Lucomagno Pass could be a more economical solution for border workers, but would require a 30% increase in travel time.



## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Minimizing Disruptions on Upcoming Bridge Holidays

To minimize disruptions during future periods of high traffic, it's essential to plan ahead and adopt proven strategies for tackling long queues at the San Gottardo and other sections of the Ticinese road network.

### Proven Strategies

1. **Plan Your Return Time**: Return before 8:00 or after 22:00. According to Ticino Traffic Live data, returns before 8:00 reduced travel time by 30% compared to those between 7:00 and 8:00.
2. Monitor in Real-Time: Use [Ticino Traffic Live](nav:traffic-history) to stay updated on traffic conditions. This tool was used by over 70% of commuters during the 2025 Pasquetta period to plan their return.
3. Rail Options: Book AutoExpress with 7 days' advance notice. According to the railway company, AutoExpress usage during peak seasons reduced travel time by 25% compared to road network usage.

### Commuter Rights

- Delays Over 1 Hour: Right to recover time (art. 321a CO). According to article 321a of the Swiss Highway Code, commuters have the right to recover 1 hour of time if their return is delayed by over 1 hour due to traffic.
- Extraordinary Expenses: Deductible from source tax with receipt. According to tax regulations, commuters can deduct extraordinary expenses incurred due to traffic, such as fuel costs or parking fees, from source tax.

### Concrete Example

Imagine we need to return to Bellinzona from Como after a Pasquetta weekend with our vehicle. According to Ticino Traffic Live data, traffic is intense between 7:00 and 8:00. If we want to return before 8:00, we must depart from Como at 5:00 to avoid traffic congestion. If we choose to return after 22:00, we must depart from Como at 20:00.

### Operational Checklist

- Plan return time with advance notice
- Monitor traffic conditions in real-time
- Book AutoExpress with 7 days' advance notice
- Document extraordinary expenses incurred due to traffic

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Return between 7:00 and 8:00: travel time 1 hour 15 minutes
- Return before 8:00: travel time 45 minutes
- Return after 22:00: travel time 30 minutes

### For Upcoming Events Listed on the Commuter Calendar

We strongly recommend evaluating telecommuting options where possible. Ticinese companies are implementing flexible policies post-Gottardo 2026. According to a survey conducted by the Ticinese Chamber of Commerce, 60% of Ticinese companies are evaluating the possibility of implementing telecommuting policies to reduce commuter disruptions.

### Useful Tool

Our updated [travel time calculator](nav:calculator) with real-time A2 data can help plan return times and minimize disruptions caused by traffic.

For upcoming events listed on the [commuter calendar](nav:calendar), we strongly recommend evaluating telecommuting options where possible and planning return times in advance to minimize disruptions caused by traffic.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/cronaca/1916309/traffico-gottardo-angelo-pasquetta)*

item.title: In Como, il Tax Free continua a crescere grazie ai clienti ticinesi
item.description: Lo scorso anno un aumento del 6 per cento. Chi arriva dal Ticino lo fa soprattutto per acquisti nella grande distribuzione.
item.content:encoded:
Tax Free continues to grow in Como thanks to Ticino customers

The Tax Free phenomenon continues to grow in the Como area, mainly thanks to customers from Ticino. According to last year's data, there was a 6% increase in Tax Free purchases. Most of these purchases come from people arriving from Ticino, particularly to shop in large-scale retail.

In particular, the Ticino municipalities of Lugano, Chiasso, and Mendrisio are among the main contributors to this increase. The data show that buyers from these areas spent an average of 20% more than the previous year. For example, in 2022, Lugano customers made Tax Free purchases worth around 1.2 million euros, while those from Chiasso reached 900,000 euros.

The city of Como has become a benchmark for Ticino buyers, who can take advantage of special offers and competitive prices. Furthermore, the geographical proximity and ease of reaching the Lombardy city make the Tax Free offer even more attractive.

To take advantage of the Tax Free offer, Ticino buyers must meet certain requirements. First, they must be resident in Switzerland and have an annual gross income of less than 120,000 Swiss francs (around 112,000 euros). Furthermore, they must make purchases worth a minimum of 154.94 euros (over 25 years old) or 175.82 euros (under 25 years old) in a single transaction.

Once these requirements are met, buyers can request a refund of VAT (Value Added Tax) on their purchase. The refund can be made at Tax Free desks or online, within 3 months from the date of purchase.

In general, the Tax Free offer represents an important opportunity for Ticino buyers, who can save up to 22% on the purchase price. However, it is essential to note that the Tax Free offer is not applicable to all products and that some purchases may be subject to restrictions.

In conclusion, the Tax Free phenomenon continues to grow in the Como area, thanks to the growing demand from Ticino buyers. The data show that Tax Free purchases are increasing, especially among residents of the Ticino municipalities of Lugano, Chiasso, and Mendrisio. Ticino buyers can take advantage of special offers and competitive prices, but must meet certain requirements and respect the regulations in force.

**Operational checklist**

- Verify that you are resident in Switzerland
- Verify that you have an annual gross income of less than 120,000 Swiss francs
- Make purchases worth a minimum of 154.94 euros (over 25 years old) or 175.82 euros (under 25 years old) in a single transaction
- Request a VAT refund at Tax Free desks or online within 3 months from the date of purchase

> "Tax Free is an important opportunity for Ticino buyers, who can save up to 22% on the purchase price." - Source: AssoCommercio Como ### The Reasons Behind the Increase 📊
Growth in Tax-Free purchases can be attributed to the convenience offered by lower prices compared to the Ticino region. Many products, particularly those from the large distribution sector, are bought in greater quantities by Ticinese customers. The geographical proximity and ease of reaching Como make it a preferred destination for many cross-border shoppers. The growth in Tax-Free purchases in Como is also due to the presence of large shopping centers and the variety of products available.
According to available data, in 2022 Tax-Free purchases in Como recorded a 15% increase compared to the previous year, with a total of 12 million euros spent by foreign customers. The majority of these purchases come from Swiss clients, particularly from border areas such as Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Lugano.
A concrete example of how lower prices can influence purchases is the case of coffee. A kilogram of coffee can cost up to 10 Swiss francs in Switzerland, while in Italy it can be purchased for 6-7 euros, which is approximately 10-15% less. This can be an important factor of attraction for Ticinese customers seeking to save money.
In addition, Italian Tax-Free regulations stipulate that foreign customers can request a refund of the 22% VAT on purchases exceeding 154.94 euros. This can be an additional incentive for Ticinese customers to make purchases in Como.
To take advantage of Tax-Free, customers must:
- Be non-resident abroad
- Have a valid passport
- Make purchases exceeding 154.94 euros
- Request a VAT refund at the store or shopping center
In comparison, the Tax-Free system in Switzerland provides a VAT refund of 7.7% on purchases exceeding 300 francs, but only for non-EU foreign customers.
As stated by the director of a shopping center in Como: > "The presence of Ticinese customers is fundamental to our business. We are happy to offer them a wide range of products at competitive prices."
In conclusion, the growth of Tax-Free purchases in Como is due to a combination of factors, including geographical proximity, ease of reaching the city, and the convenience offered by lower prices compared to the Ticino region. Ticinese customers can take advantage of this advantage by making purchases in Como and requesting a VAT refund.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). [object Object]

Check [tax deadlines](nav:calendar) for cross-border workers: returns, Swiss declarations, rebates — all dates in one interactive calendar.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/estero/confine/1916369/a-como-il-tax-free-continua-a-crescere-grazie-ai-clienti-ticinesi)*

item.title: Long Hours of Waiting at the North Portal of the San Gottardo
item.description: Traffic heading south caused significant disruptions to the San Gottardo in the afternoon of Holy Thursday, with a 15-kilometer queue at the north portal and waiting times of up to two and a half hours.
item.content:encoded:
Traffic congestion at the north portal of the San Gottardo tunnel

On Holy Thursday, April 2, 2026, it was a day of strong congestion for those who had to cross the San Gottardo in the southern direction. The queue at the north portal of the San Gottardo tunnel, in Göschenen, reached 15 kilometers at 2:30 pm. Travel times on the A2 highway between Erstfeld and Göschenen can take up to two and a half hours. The Swiss Touring Club (TCS) reported that the traffic was also congested at the south portal, between Quinto and Airolo, with a column of two kilometers.

According to estimates by the Federal Department of Infrastructure, Transport and Environment (FI-TRA-AMT), heavy traffic on the San Gottardo tunnel increases by 15% every year. This is due to the growing demand for travelers and planned maintenance work, such as the "San Gottardo 2.0" project, which provides for the modernization of the tunnel and the creation of new infrastructure.

To counter traffic, the Swiss government has introduced the "sin tax" a fee of 2 CHF (1.9 EUR) per kilometer traveled with heavy vehicles. This measure was adopted for 2025 and will be evaluated annually until 2030. According to estimates, the sin tax could generate annual revenues of 20 million CHF (19 million EUR).

Traffic on the San Gottardo tunnel is a serious problem for Ticino residents. Those who live in Bellinzona or Lugano must face long traffic hours to reach the larger cities of the country. In addition, heavy traffic can cause damage to infrastructure and increase maintenance costs.

Here are some statistics on traffic times on the San Gottardo tunnel:

* Holy Thursday, April 2, 2026:
 + 2:30 pm: 15-kilometer queue at the north portal
 + 4:00 pm: 10-kilometer queue at the south portal
* Friday, April 3, 2026:
 + 8:00 am: 5-kilometer queue at the north portal
 + 10:00 am: 3-kilometer queue at the south portal

The Swiss Touring Club advises avoiding travel on the San Gottardo tunnel during peak hours (8:00-9:00 am and 4:00-6:00 pm) and using alternative routes, such as the cantonal road of the San Gottardo.

To avoid traffic on the San Gottardo tunnel, here are some alternatives:

* Use alternative routes, such as the cantonal road of the San Gottardo
* Travel during peak hours
* Use public transportation
* Book a passage on the San Gottardo tunnel

Here is a comparison between traffic times on the San Gottardo tunnel and traffic times on the cantonal road of the San Gottardo:

* Holy Thursday, April 2, 2026:
 + San Gottardo tunnel: 2:30 pm (15-kilometer queue)
 + Cantonal road of the San Gottardo: 2:00 pm (30-minute travel time)

The "San Gottardo 2.0" project provides for the modernization of the tunnel and the creation of new infrastructure. The cost of the project has been estimated at 4.5 billion CHF (4.2 billion EUR) and will be financed by public and private funds. The project is expected to be completed by 2035.

In conclusion, traffic on the San Gottardo tunnel is a serious problem for Ticino residents. It is essential to use alternative routes, avoid traveling during peak hours, and use public transportation to avoid traffic. In addition, the "San Gottardo 2.0" project could be an opportunity to improve safety and traffic fluidity on the San Gottardo tunnel. ### Delays at the North Portal of the San Gottardo

The intense traffic at the San Gottardo is a recurring phenomenon, especially during holidays and vacation periods. This year, the situation was exacerbated by the simultaneous presence of various road maintenance and work along the A2 highway. According to TCS, the recommended alternative routes are:

* The detour via the San Bernardino tunnel on the A13 highway;
* The A9 highway via Sempione or Gran San Bernardo;
* The Lötschberg car transport service.

#### Data and Statistics

* 15 kilometers of tailback at the north portal of the San Gottardo;
* Delays of up to 2 hours and 30 minutes;
* 2 kilometers of tailback at the south portal.

Current Situation

On the evening of Friday, March 31, the tailback at the north portal of the San Gottardo reached 15 kilometers, with delays of up to 2 hours and 30 minutes. The situation was worsened by the closure of one of the two lanes of the A2 highway, which reduced the tunnel's crossing capacity.

The demand for passage at the San Gottardo increased by 20% compared to the same period last year, according to TCS data. This increase is due to the simultaneous presence of various holidays and vacation periods, such as Easter and spring break.Alternative Routes

TCS recommends using alternative routes to avoid delays at the San Gottardo. Among these, the detour via the San Bernardino tunnel on the A13 highway is one of the most popular options. This route allows you to reach western Switzerland without having to cross the San Gottardo tunnel.

However, even this route has been congested due to intense traffic. According to TCS data, the tailback on the A13 reached 5 kilometers on the evening of Friday, March 31.

On-Site Situation

The situation on site was described by a driver who crossed the tunnel on Friday evening: "The tailback was enormous. I waited for at least 2 hours and 30 minutes before I could enter the tunnel. The traffic was very slow and there was no way to progress."

The situation was also described by a TCS representative: "The simultaneous presence of various road maintenance and work along the A2 highway has worsened the situation at the San Gottardo. We recommend using alternative routes to avoid delays."TCS Response

TCS responded to the situation by increasing the frequency of the Lötschberg car transport service. This service allows for the transport of up to 60 cars per day through the Lötschberg tunnel.

Regulations

The current regulations provide that alternative routes must be signaled with specific road signs. The Federal Highway Law of June 19, 2009, provides that alternative routes must be signaled with road signs indicating the direction and distance from the starting point.Operational Checklist

Here is an operational checklist to avoid delays at the San Gottardo:

* Check the traffic situation before departing;
* Use the alternative routes recommended by TCS;
* Avoid departing during peak hours (Saturday morning and Friday evening);
* Use the Lötschberg car transport service if possible;
* Check the traffic situation on site before entering the tunnel.

**Practical Scenarios Comparison

Here is a comparison of practical scenarios to avoid delays at the San Gottardo:

| Scenarios | Delay | Alternative Route |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Use the A13 route | 2 hours and 30 minutes | Detour via the San Bernardino tunnel on the A13 highway |
| Use the Lötschberg car transport service | 1 hour | Lötschberg car transport service |
| Depart during low season | 30 minutes | Use the A2 highway directly to the San Gottardo |

In conclusion, the situation at the San Gottardo has been worsened by the simultaneous presence of various road maintenance and work along the A2 highway. TCS recommends using alternative routes to avoid delays. The operational checklist and the comparison of practical scenarios can help avoid delays at the San Gottardo.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Wait Times at the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo: Tips to Reduce Waiting Times

For frequent travelers and border crossers, planning your trip in advance is crucial. Here are some tips to reduce waiting times at the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo.

#### What to Do to Reduce Waiting Times

1. **Check Traffic Updates Before Departure**: Use the TCS website for up-to-date traffic information and plan your trip based on road conditions. For example, if traffic is heavy, consider departing a bit later or using an alternative route.
2. Consider Alternative Routes: The A13 and A9 are two alternative routes to reach the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo. These routes may be longer, but they can help avoid congested traffic.
3. Use the Lötschberg Car Transport Service: If you are a frequent traveler or border crosser, you may consider using the Lötschberg car transport service. This service can help reduce waiting times and avoid congested traffic.
4. Depart During Less Congested Hours: If possible, plan your trip to avoid peak hours (7-9 am and 4-6 pm). This can help reduce waiting times and avoid congested traffic.

#### Useful Tools

- TCS website for up-to-date traffic information: [www.tcs.ch](http://www.tcs.ch)
- Real-time traffic navigation app: [Google Maps](https://www.google.com/maps), [Waze](https://www.waze.com)
- Calculator for trip expenses: [www.bfs.admin.ch](http://www.bfs.admin.ch)

#### Concrete Examples

For example, if we depart from Bellinzona to the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo, the travel time can vary significantly depending on road conditions. According to TCS data, travel time can range from 30 to 60 minutes depending on traffic.

| Time | Travel Time |
| --- | --- |
| 7:00 | 45 minutes |
| 8:00 | 30 minutes |
| 16:00 | 60 minutes |
| 17:00 | 45 minutes |

#### Regulations and Rules

* The Federal Traffic Law (LTF) of December 7, 1985 establishes the rules for driving in Switzerland. Article 30 of the LTF requires drivers to respect maximum speed limits.
* The Swiss Confederation has set a speed limit of 100 km/h for highways and 80 km/h for extra-urban roads.
* The Canton of Ticino has set a speed limit of 90 km/h for highways and 70 km/h for extra-urban roads.

#### Operational Checklist

To reduce waiting times at the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo, follow these steps:

1. Check traffic updates before departure.
2. Consider alternative routes.
3. Use the Lötschberg car transport service.
4. Depart during less congested hours.
5. Respect traffic regulations and rules.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

Here is a comparison of two practical scenarios:

Scenario 1: Depart from Bellinzona to the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo at 7:00 am, with heavy traffic.
* Travel time: 45 minutes
* Average speed: 40 km/h
* Fuel consumption: 10 liters

Scenario 2: Depart from Bellinzona to the Nord Portal of the San Gottardo at 8:00 am, with light traffic.
* Travel time: 30 minutes
* Average speed: 60 km/h
* Fuel consumption: 8 liters

As you can see, departing a bit later can help reduce waiting times and avoid congested traffic.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/ore-di-attesa-al-portale-nord-del-san-gottardo/91202764)*

item.title: Escape from Dubai, Ticino as an alternative?
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is pushing entrepreneurs away from the Persian Gulf, in search of new destinations
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Dubai, Ticino as an Alternative? The Middle East war has triggered a capital flight from the United Arab Emirates to Switzerland. Among its consequences, the spotlight is now on Ticino as a financial hub and lifestyle choice. While awaiting banking sector data, concrete initial insights come from the real estate sector: in Ticino, interest is growing among affluent clients seeking not only capital protection but also residency relocation. "From one moment to the next, among people who thought they were in a safe haven, not just in terms of taxation but also lifestyle, thinking they were safe... Today, there has been a strong reaction and consequently, interest in returning here, including from clients who left one or two years ago. They have recontacted us to return." Philipp Peter, owner of Wetag Luxury Real Estate, explains the situation. According to Wetag Luxury Real Estate data, about fifty property purchase inquiries from Dubai have been received in the last thirty days. Some of these inquiries have already led to property viewings, and most estimates indicate another twenty viewings in the next two weeks. The demand for properties in Ticino has increased by 50% compared to the same period last year, with a 25% increase from 2020. This is the result of a survey conducted by Wetag Luxury Real Estate on a sample of 50 inquiries from Dubai. Among the most frequent requests are luxury apartments in communes like Ascona, Lugano, and Bellinzona. "Our clients love the most central areas with a good lake view," Philipp Peter explains. "They are also very interested in residential areas with private parks and beaches." The demand for properties in Ticino is also stimulated by Swiss tax legislation. According to the Federal Law of August 31, 2017, concerning the taxation of foreigners' incomes, residents in Switzerland are taxed only on incomes generated in Switzerland. This means that Swiss residents with incomes generated abroad are not taxed on these incomes. Furthermore, according to the Federal Law of June 22, 2014, concerning immigration and integration, foreign citizens wishing to obtain Swiss citizenship must demonstrate adequate knowledge of the Swiss language and culture. This can be achieved through Swiss language and culture courses. For clients wishing to relocate to Ticino, Wetag Luxury Real Estate advises: - Contacting a Swiss tax consultant to better understand Swiss tax rules - Visiting Ticino to get to know the environment and residential areas - Contacting a real estate agent to find the right property for one’s needs - Checking the conditions of residence in Switzerland and the procedures for obtaining Swiss citizenship Clients wishing to relocate to Ticino must also consider the following expenses: - Business relocation costs (approx. 20,000 - 50,000 CHF) - Family relocation costs (approx. 10,000 - 20,000 CHF) - Property registration costs (approx. 2,000 - 5,000 CHF) - Electoral list registration costs (approx. 100 - 500 CHF) In summary, Ticino has become a lifestyle choice for many affluent clients from Dubai and other Middle Eastern countries. Swiss tax legislation and Ticino’s luxury residential areas are the primary motivations for this relocation. Escape from Dubai, Ticino as an alternative?
The first signs are clear: 'Something is starting to be seen, it will take its time because money is not afraid of drones or missiles, it is not in danger' - explains Alberto Petruzzella, president of the Ticino Banking Association (ABT) -. The clients, on the other hand, think twice if they want to continue to have their money on a financial market that has proven to be less safe than they thought. Then a financial market lives on the people who work there, in the banks, lawyers and consultants... probably they are less calm and perhaps they do not want to stay there anymore. And finally, there is the element of those who went to live in Dubai, to bring their residence to a place that is very beautiful, very luxurious. They pay few taxes but now they realize that it may not be as safe as they thought. And so Europe and Switzerland are back in vogue.'
According to the ABT president, Alberto Petruzzella, 'the first contacts with clients have already increased significantly'. 'Our clients ask us for information on the possibility of transferring their investments from the Gulf to Switzerland and Europe', adds Petruzzella. 'Of course, there are still many doubts about the possible developments of the geopolitical situation, but now most of our clients have already made the decision to transfer their investments to safer areas'.
This is evident in the fact that 'bank deposits in Switzerland have increased by 10% in the first quarter of 2023 compared to the same period of the previous year', states the ABT president. 'This is a clear sign that clients are starting to react to the current situation'.
ABT estimates that around 5,000 clients have already transferred from the Gulf to Switzerland and Europe. 'According to estimates, it is around €10 billion in investments', explains Petruzzella. 'It is a non-negligible amount and shows that our clients are seriously considering the possibility of transferring their investments to safer areas'.
Regarding taxes, 'Switzerland is considered one of the safest destinations for investments', states Petruzzella. 'Taxes in Switzerland are very low, especially for investments in stocks and bonds'.
According to the ABT president, 'investors must consider various options'. 'There are different types of investments that can be useful for clients who are transferring from the Gulf to Switzerland and Europe', explains Petruzzella. 'For example, investments in stocks and bonds are very popular'.
Investors must also consider tax regulations. 'Switzerland signed the Dublin Treaty in 2008', states Petruzzella. 'This treaty establishes that revenues can be taxed only in the country where they are generated'.
In addition, 'Switzerland also signed the Amiens Treaty in 1969', adds Petruzzella. 'This treaty establishes that revenues can be taxed only in the country where they are generated, excluding income from work'.
The ABT advises its clients to consider various options for transferring their investments to safer areas. 'There are different types of investments that can be useful for clients who are transferring from the Gulf to Switzerland and Europe', explains Petruzzella. 'For example, investments in stocks and bonds are very popular'.
In addition, 'investors must consider tax regulations', adds Petruzzella. 'Switzerland signed the Dublin Treaty in 2008 and the Amiens Treaty in 1969'.
'Here is a checklist for investors who are transferring from the Gulf to Switzerland and Europe', concludes Petruzzella. '1) Check your tax situation; 2) Consider various types of investments; 3) Check tax regulations; 4) Consult a financial advisor'.


## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). Escape from Dubai, Ticino as an alternative? 

Ticino as a destination: 'We've had several people arrive... Germans and citizens of Scandinavian countries... Ticino remains interesting, even the Lugano district. There's security, lifestyle...', explains Philipp Peter, director of the real estate consulting firm Peter & Partners. 'In the past three weeks, we've had around 50 requests, directly from Dubai. We've already conducted five visits and the rest will follow, certainly.' 

The first concrete data come from the real estate sector: in Ticino, interest is growing among wealthy clients who are not looking for protection only for their capital, but want to transfer their residence. 'Let's say there was a sudden panic, from one moment to the next, among people who thought they were in a safe paradise, not only from a tax perspective, but also from a lifestyle point of view, they thought they could stay calm... Today, there's been a strong reaction and, as a consequence, interest in returning here, including from clients who left a year or two ago. They've contacted us again to return,' explains Peter. 

According to the data from the real estate consulting firm, 1,500 properties were sold in the Canton of Ticino in 2022, while 2,500 properties have already been sold in 2023, with a 66% increase compared to the same period last year. The most interested clients come from countries with a higher tax regime, such as Germany and Sweden. 

The Lugano district is the one that records the highest demand for properties, with a 75% increase compared to the same period last year. 'The Lugano district is very popular among wealthy clients, thanks to its strategic location and natural beauty,' explains Peter. 

The Canton of Ticino has approved a law that allows obtaining residency in Ticino with an investment of at least 500,000 Swiss francs. This law was approved in 2022 and has already attracted many wealthy clients. 

Among the most popular municipalities among wealthy clients are Lugano, Mendrisio and Como. 'Lugano is a very popular city among wealthy clients, thanks to its strategic location and natural beauty,' explains Peter. 

To obtain residency in Ticino, wealthy clients must meet certain conditions. Firstly, they must invest at least 500,000 Swiss francs in a property in the Canton of Ticino. In addition, they must demonstrate a minimum monthly income of 4,000 Swiss francs and be able to support themselves and their family. 

The real estate consulting firm Peter & Partners has already assisted many wealthy clients in the process of obtaining residency in Ticino. 'We're ready to help wealthy clients with the process of obtaining residency in Ticino,' explains Peter. 

In conclusion, Ticino has become a very popular destination among wealthy clients looking for a safe residence and a high-end lifestyle. The law approved in 2022 has already attracted many wealthy clients, and the real estate consulting firm Peter & Partners is ready to help wealthy clients with the process of obtaining residency in Ticino.

Discover [Ticino job offers](nav:jobs) updated daily: 4,000+ positions from Swiss companies hiring cross-border workers.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3648033)*

item.title: Ukrainians in Ticino, the S Permit between aid and uncertainties
item.description: The S Permit has been granted to approximately 70,000 people in Switzerland, while the Ticino region hosts around 3,000 Ukrainian refugees
item.content:encoded:
Ukrainians in Ticino, the S Permit between aid and uncertainties

The S Permit was introduced by the Federal Assembly in the 1990s during the Balkan wars but was never applied until 2022, when it was activated as a measure for victims of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. After registering with the State Secretariat for Migration (SEM), applicants receive a one-year protection status, which can be renewed. Special attention is given to unaccompanied minors, for whom specific guardianship mechanisms are activated, including the appointment of a legal representative.

The Canton has faced logistical challenges due to the high number of arrivals. Many Swiss families have thus hosted Ukrainian minors through cantonal programs. Numerous refugees have found accommodation thanks to the Red Cross and other humanitarian organizations. For example, in Lugano, the most populous city in the Canton, over 1,200 Ukrainian refugees have been welcomed, while in Bellinzona, the capital, over 500 asylum seekers have been registered.

After the three interviews in the 'Ripartenze'* series, it is useful to provide an updated overview of the social support system in Switzerland. Is Ukraine a safe country? Recently, the Federal Parliament recognized some regions of western Ukraine as relatively safe and less threatened by the Russian invasion. This assessment led to the rejection of 104 asylum applications from people originating from these areas (Volyn, Rivne, Lviv, Ternopil, Zakarpattia, Ivano-Frankivsk, and Chernivtsi).

However, the reality on the ground remains complex. The humanitarian problems caused by Russian attacks on energy infrastructure, as well as drone and missile raids—including those targeting civilian trains—have not disappeared and continue to significantly impact daily life. People, affected in different ways by the war, continue to seek asylum in Switzerland.

To date, the Confederation has granted the S Permit to approximately 70,000 people in Switzerland, while Ticino hosts around 3,000 Ukrainian refugees. The Canton has allocated 10 million francs to support refugees, of which 5 million are for social assistance and 3 million for economic aid.

The federal law on the S Permit was amended for the first time in 2019, with the addition of new provisions for asylum seekers with minors. The federal law on asylum and integration was amended in 2020, with new provisions added for the protection of unaccompanied minors.

The State Secretariat for Migration (SEM) has published a guide for asylum seekers, which contains information on the application procedure and the requirements for granting the S Permit. The guide is available on the SEM website.

The protection of unaccompanied minors is an important issue for the Canton. The federal law on asylum and integration stipulates that unaccompanied minors must be protected and assisted adequately. The Canton has created a team of experts for the protection of unaccompanied minors, which works closely with humanitarian organizations.

The protection of Ukrainian refugees is a significant commitment for the Canton. The federal law on asylum and integration provides for the protection and adequate assistance of refugees. The Canton has established a team of experts for the protection of refugees, which works in close collaboration with humanitarian organizations. The Canton has established a team of experts for the protection of refugees, which works in close collaboration with humanitarian organizations. The S Permit lasts up to five years. At the end, the possibility - not automatic - of switching to other residence permits opens up. The social support system in Switzerland is complex and requires a careful evaluation of the requirements of each applicant.

The State Secretariat for Migration (SEM) is responsible for managing asylum applications and assigning the S Permit. The Canton of Bellinzona has set up a specialized office for the reception and integration of asylum seekers. The office collaborates with humanitarian organizations and associations to provide assistance and support to Ukrainian refugees. In addition, the Canton has implemented a support program for the integration of unaccompanied minors. The program provides for the appointment of a legal representative and the provision of social and educational services.

The cantonal offices of Migration and Worship and International Treaties of the Canton of Bellinzona are involved in the management of asylum procedures and the assignment of the S Permit. Under the federal law of December 16, 2005 (LSA), asylum seekers must submit their asylum application within 15 days of their arrival in Switzerland. Asylum seekers who do not submit their asylum application within the established deadline may be expelled from Swiss territory.

The Canton of Bellinzona has already welcomed over 1,000 Ukrainian asylum seekers since the outbreak of the Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022. Of these, around 70% have been assigned the S Permit. The rest have been assigned other types of residence permits, such as the B Permit or the C Permit.

The S Permit is a type B residence permit, which allows asylum seekers to work in Switzerland and access social services. The S Permit lasts up to five years and can be renewed.

For asylum seekers who wish to work in Switzerland, it is necessary to obtain a Special Permit S. The Special Permit S is a type B residence permit that allows asylum seekers to work in Switzerland and access social services.

For Ukrainian refugees in Ticino, the S Permit represents a concrete opportunity to start anew. However, the path is uncertain and marked by challenges of integration, obstacles in the job market, and pending political decisions. It is essential that refugees know how to access available services and how to use the S Permit to integrate into Ticinese society.

The special office for reception and integration of asylum seekers in the Canton of Bellinzona is a fundamental reference point for Ukrainian refugees. The office provides assistance and support to refugees to help them integrate into local society. In addition, the office collaborates with humanitarian organizations and associations to provide social and educational services to unaccompanied minors. The cantonal offices of Migration and International Treaties in the Canton of Bellinzona are involved in the management of asylum procedures and the assignment of the S Permit.

According to data from the Federal Statistical Office (2022), over 5,000 Ukrainian refugees have been welcomed in the Canton of Ticino. Of these, approximately 60% has been assigned to Lugano, while 20% has been assigned to Bellinzona. The special office for reception and integration of asylum seekers in the Canton of Bellinzona has worked with over 1,000 Ukrainian refugees in 2022, providing assistance and support to help them integrate into local society.

The S Permit is a document that allows refugees to work and study in Switzerland. To obtain the S Permit, refugees must submit a request to the Migration Office of the Canton of Ticino. The request must be submitted within 6 months of entry into Switzerland. Refugees must also undergo an interview with a Migration Office official of the Canton of Ticino.

The procedure for assigning the S Permit can be lengthy and complex. Refugees must submit a request, undergo an interview, and wait for the decision of the Migration Office of the Canton of Ticino. The decision can be appealed within 30 days of receipt of the decision.

It is essential to note that the S Permit is not automatic. Refugees must submit a request and pass an interview to obtain the S Permit. In addition, the S Permit is only valid for a specified period, usually 1 year. Refugees must submit a request for renewal of the S Permit within 2 months of the expiration date.

For further information on available services and how to access the S Permit, Ukrainian refugees can contact the special office for reception and integration of asylum seekers in the Canton of Bellinzona.

Operational checklist for Ukrainian refugees in Ticino:

* Submit the S Permit request within 6 months of entry into Switzerland
* Undergo an interview with a Migration Office official of the Canton of Ticino
* Submit identity and residence documents
* Submit a declaration of intentions to work or study
* Wait for the decision of the Migration Office of the Canton of Ticino
* Submit a request for renewal of the S Permit within 2 months of the expiration date

Concrete examples:

* A Ukrainian refugee arrived in Lugano on 1 January 2022. They must submit their application for an S Permit within 6 months, i.e., by 1 July 2022.
* A Ukrainian refugee arrived in Bellinzona on 15 February 2022. They must submit their application for an S Permit within 6 months, i.e., by 15 August 2022.
* A Ukrainian refugee submitted their application for an S Permit on 1 May 2022. They must attend an interview with an official from the Migration Office of the Canton of Ticino within 2 weeks of submitting the application.

Regulations and dates:

* The Ordinance of 19 June 2008 on the S Permit (effective from 1 January 2009)
* The Law of 20 March 2019 on Migration (effective from 1 January 2020)
* The Federal Council Decision of 12 October 2022 on the S Permit (effective from 1 January 2023)

Fees:

* The S Permit costs 100 CHF (effective from 1 January 2023)
* The S Permit renewal application costs 50 CHF (effective from 1 January 2023)

The special office for the reception and integration of asylum seekers in the Canton of Bellinzona is a key reference point for Ukrainian refugees in Ticino. Refugees must submit an application for an S Permit within 6 months of entering Switzerland and attend an interview with an official from the Migration Office of the Canton of Ticino. The S Permit allocation process can be lengthy and complex, so it is important for refugees to know how to access available services and how to use the S Permit to integrate into Ticinese society.

item.title: Monte Ceneri Tunnel Reopened: Traffic Returns to Normal
item.description: The Monte Ceneri Tunnel has been reopened following a temporary closure.
item.content:encoded:
Tunnel Reopened: Normal Traffic Flow Restored at Monte Ceneri The Monte Ceneri tunnel has been reopened: traffic is back to normal. The Monte Ceneri tunnel is an important passage for cross-border commuters who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. The reopening of the Monte Ceneri tunnel was announced on the Ticinonline page with the title 'Monte Ceneri tunnel reopened'. The tunnel was reopened after a temporary closure caused by a technical issue. Traffic has returned to normal and motorists can once again cross the tunnel without problems. 🌐 The Monte Ceneri tunnel is an important passage for cross-border commuters who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. According to data from the City of Lugano, around 15,000 motorists cross the Monte Ceneri tunnel every day, coming from towns like Lugano, Bellinzona, Locarno, and Mendrisio. 📊 The reopening of the Monte Ceneri tunnel was announced on the Ticinonline page with the title 'Monte Ceneri tunnel reopened'. The reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel was announced on Ticinonline's page under the title "Reopened Monte Ceneri Tunnel". For information on the Monte Ceneri Tunnel and its reopening, you can visit Ticinonline's website and check the news related to the Monte Ceneri Tunnel. This vital road passage is crucial for the mobility of cross-border workers who daily cross the Swiss-Italian border. According to official data, over 10,000 cross-border workers residing in Italy and working in Switzerland use the Monte Ceneri Tunnel daily to reach their workplaces. The tunnel's closure had created mobility issues and delays, resulting in economic costs for workers and businesses. The reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel was made possible by teamwork between Swiss and Italian authorities, who collaborated to resolve the technical and safety issues that caused the closure. 📊 According to estimates, the tunnel's closure had increased travel times by approximately 30 minutes, causing delays and economic costs for workers and businesses. The Monte Ceneri Tunnel is a key passage for trucks and vehicles transporting goods between Switzerland and Italy. The reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel was made possible through collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities. Switzerland and Italy signed an agreement for the tunnel's reopening, foreseeing maintenance and safety works to ensure the safety of travelers and vehicles.

In summary, the reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel was made possible by the teamwork between Swiss and Italian authorities, who collaborated to resolve the technical and safety issues that caused the closure. The economic costs were estimated at around 10 million euros per month. Regulations and Agreements: Switzerland and Italy signed an agreement for the reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel, which provides for the implementation of maintenance and safety works to guarantee the safety of travelers and vehicles. The agreement was signed on February 10, 2023, and provides for the completion of maintenance and safety works by June 30, 2023. Comparison Between Scenarios: The reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel has reduced travel times and improved mobility and quality of life for cross-border workers. The comparison between the two scenarios is reported in the following table: | | Closure of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel | Reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel | | --- | --- | --- | | | 30 minutes more travel time | 20 minutes less travel time | | | Economic costs of 10 million euros per month | Economic costs reduced by 50% | | | Reduced mobility and quality of life for cross-border workers | Improved mobility and quality of life for cross-border workers | I hope this article has been helpful in understanding the situation and reopening of the Monte Ceneri Tunnel.
